mnemonics.title: Array Index Starts at 0
mnemonics.content: Think 'Zeroth' element - arrays start at ZERO, not one. Remember: O in 'Zero' stands for 'Origin' of indexing.

mnemonics.title: Array Length Property
mnemonics.content: Remember: LENGTH is a property (no parentheses), not a METHOD. Like 'arr.color' - it's an attribute of the array object.

mnemonics.title: Default Values Memory
mnemonics.content: 0 for numbers, false for boolean, null for objects, '\u0000' for char - Think: 'Number FoOLS have No power'

acronyms.usage: Executes Java bytecode and manages memory for arrays
Arrays class provides utility methods in java.util.Arrays API

visualTips.visualization: Think of an array as a hotel with numbered rooms starting from room 0. Each room (index) holds one guest (element). You can directly go to room 5 without checking previous rooms.
A 2D array is like an Excel spreadsheet - rows and columns. Row 0, Column 0 is the top-left cell. Each cell holds one value.
Visualize arrays as contiguous memory blocks - like train cars connected together. Each car (element) is next to the other, allowing fast random access.

analogies.analogy: Declaring an array is like reserving a block of hotel rooms before guests arrive. You decide how many rooms (size) but don't fill them yet.
Initializing with values is like checking guests into their reserved rooms - each room gets a specific person (value).
Array index is like a house address. House number 0 is the first house. You can find anyone quickly if you know their house number (index).
